headline: Figma's AI Tokens Are Live
subheadline: Figma's AI design tokens hit general availability — huge for your white-label design system work. Plus Cursor's security agents, Claude Opus 4.6, and the shift from chatbots to agentic AI in design systems.

sections.label: Trending
sections.title: Figma AI Design Tokens hit General Availability
sections.description: This is directly in your wheelhouse. Figma's AI now suggests color, spacing, and typography tokens based on existing codebases — and writes them. For your white-label sport apps design system with MCP + Claude, this could dramatically accelerate how you generate and maintain tokens across brand variants. Worth digging into how this plays with your existing variables setup.

sections.label: Read
sections.title: Figma's AI Design Development MCP — two-way UI to code workflows
sections.description: Figma just dropped MCP support enabling two-way design-to-code workflows across Cursor, Warp, Factory, and others. You're already running Figma MCP with Claude — this expands the bridge significantly. The admin AI credits system and AI collaboration features are also new. Check the release notes for what's changed since your last setup.

sections.label: Read
sections.title: Cursor's March update: security automation agents and MCP
sections.description: Cursor released four security automation templates — agentic security review, vulnerability hunting, and automated PR repair across projects. If you're vibe-coding and shipping real things, having automated security checks baked into your workflow is the kind of guardrail that matters as your projects get more complex.

sections.label: Concept of the day
sections.title: Agentic AI in Design Systems — beyond chatbots
sections.description: The biggest shift in enterprise design systems this year: moving from 'ask AI to generate something' to AI agents that autonomously maintain, audit, and evolve your system. Think agents that detect token drift, flag inconsistencies across white-label themes, or propose component updates. This is the trajectory your Figma MCP + Claude project is already on.

sections.label: Read
sections.title: Claude Opus 4.6 and Claude Cowork: what's new from Anthropic
sections.description: Opus 4.6 brings stronger coding, better planning and debugging — all directly relevant to your Claude Code workflow. Claude Cowork (the desktop agent that reads, creates, and manages files locally) is now being customized for enterprise. If your company starts exploring Claude beyond your personal setup, this is the path it'll take.

sections.label: Watch
sections.title: Claude Code is all you need in 2026
sections.description: A practical look at building with Claude Code as the single tool — no elaborate frameworks or custom setups. With 91k views and a clear thesis that the toolchain is simplifying, this validates the direction you're heading. Good for seeing how others structure their MCP servers and hooks in practice.

sections.label: Quick tip
sections.title: Define MCP servers in .mcp.json for persistent Claude Code setups
sections.description: If you haven't already, check out this dev's Claude Code setup — he runs it 24/7 on a VPS with MCP servers defined in `.mcp.json`, plus hooks and skills files. For your Figma MCP integration, structuring your servers this way means Claude can pick up context automatically without you re-explaining your design system every session.

sections.label: Think about this
sections.title: If AI agents can maintain design systems, what does a Head of Design actually lead?
sections.description: With AI tokens auto-suggesting, agents auditing consistency, and MCP bridging design-to-code — the tactical work of design systems is being automated fast. As someone who's been Group Head of Design, the interesting question becomes: does this free design leaders to focus on product strategy and brand vision, or does it collapse the role entirely? Worth sitting with as you think about your next career move.
